121303.explanation: An exact 50/50 split is not a goal in itself, but a good starting point. Quality is always the most important. With a woman as lead candidate, it certainly won't be for lack of effort from Gemeentebelangen!

121304.explanation: History shows that complex issues often do not lend themselves to a simple yes-or-no question in a referendum. A balance between the public interest and individual interest is made by the 31 democratically elected council members.

121307.explanation: Cafés and restaurants contribute to a lively city. However, other interests, such as those of surrounding residents, must be taken into account.

121308.explanation: Financial support for climate measures is a task for the national government, not from the municipal budget. We do support residents with low incomes to take energy-saving measures, thereby helping them reduce their monthly expenses AND doing something good for the climate.

121310.explanation: Asylum reception is national policy; the municipality implements the law.

121314.explanation: In response to the national fireworks ban, this could be a nice alternative to bring the city together.

121315.explanation: The municipality incurs costs here; if these are not paid by the perpetrator, they must be recovered by other means, for example via the property tax. We choose to have the perpetrator pay.

121322.explanation: Sports clubs receive (youth) sports subsidies based on their number of members. If a club needs additional resources, each situation must be assessed for suitability and financial stability.

121325.explanation: Safety is an important theme, but efforts should be moderate and focused on places where a balance between privacy and safety can be made.

121327.explanation: We believe in supervision and enforcement with a strong focus on de-escalation. The use of the baton does not align with this idea. However, we do not exclude it in the future if societal hardening calls for it.

121332.explanation: The option to convert offices into housing must be facilitated by the municipality. The financial investment (and thus the return) lies with the project developers.

121333.explanation: There is a housing shortage, so we must build more and faster.

121334.explanation: This is fair and also promotes a return to regular work (reintegration).

121336.explanation: We will continue to support the poorest residents, reduce bureaucratic pressure, and act more from trust.

121341.explanation: The center must be a healthy, green space where healthy air quality is of great importance.

121342.explanation: Due to labor market scarcity, this is no longer a realistic assumption. (It is also not a legal right.)

121346.explanation: Combating loneliness, and the costs of collection and enforcement hardly weigh against the revenues.

121347.explanation: If visitors from outside our municipality come to enjoy our facilities, a tourist tax may fairly be charged.

121350.explanation: Asylum reception is national policy; the municipality implements the law.

121351.explanation: The municipality incurs costs here; if not paid by the perpetrator, they must be recovered by other means, for example via the property tax. We choose to have the perpetrator pay.

121352.explanation: Can be combined with nature development. (By the way, this is not an issue in the municipality; Oosterhout already meets targets with the permitted solar parks.)

121353.explanation: National policy is against the introduction of school swimming, and most schools are also opposed. At the same time, we consider swimming education very important. (Note for editors: Oosterhout currently does not provide this either, so the statement is factually incorrect; it will continue to be stated.)

121354.explanation: We follow national policy but do not want to support an attraction to drug crime and addiction.

121355.explanation: Yes, this is already existing policy. (Note for editors: the statement suggests this is currently not the case, but it is.)

121360.explanation: The center must be a green, healthy place to stay, where fewer rather than more cars in the streetscape are preferable.

121362.explanation: This causes a lot of nuisance and dangerous situations.

121363.explanation: Building should be based on need, so for starters, but also for seniors (who often leave behind a larger single-family home that a young family can then occupy).

121365.explanation: Asylum reception is national policy; the municipality implements the law. We find the scale of 250 too large; we prefer it to be spread across the municipality in smaller numbers.

121303.explanation: The executive board must be a good reflection of society.

121304.explanation: There is a referendum regulation in Oosterhout that we want to keep. Additionally, we want residents to participate in other ways regarding important decisions, for example through citizen assemblies.

121307.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to keep the opening hours of horeca establishments in the center as they are now. Having the same opening hours allows for better focus on safety. In neighborhoods, we look at each horeca establishment to determine appropriate closing times due to potential nuisance for residents.

121308.explanation: We encourage initiatives from residents to generate green electricity through ONE or new energy cooperatives and to, for example, install a neighborhood battery.

121310.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports a home for everyone. We stand behind the Oosterhout migration plan and the distribution law. Therefore, we take responsibility for our share in the housing challenge for refugees.

121314.explanation: Unfortunately, private fireworks caused too many injuries and damages. From 2026 onwards, a nationwide fireworks ban will apply. A fireworks show on New Year's Eve can be a good alternative for this. This could also be a light show.

121315.explanation: Bulky waste must be collected at home, and this should be done cost-effectively. This also lowers the threshold for proper recycling.

121322.explanation: Sports clubs are the place to meet each other and have a chat. A good sports club brings an entire village or neighborhood together. Therefore, the municipality provides good, sustainable sports fields and halls for a limited amount. We want to keep it that way.

121325.explanation: It is very important to feel safe on the streets. However, we do not see more cameras in the center and residential areas as the solution. Your privacy is a great value that should not be compromised easily. Therefore, we prefer more enforcers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

121327.explanation: As far as GroenLinks-PvdA is concerned, only the police may use force when absolutely unavoidable.

121332.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports converting vacant offices into homes. It is possible to convert office buildings without subsidies, like the old ING office. Developers must comply with the rule that at least 80% of the homes are affordable.

121333.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports stimulating student and youth housing in Oosterhout. If Oosterhout wants to house students, we must make it as attractive as possible. We do this not with container homes but with permanent student housing.

121334.explanation: The municipality must approach residents with trust. Moreover, work can never be paid with benefits; work must be paid as work. Therefore, we also help residents find appropriate daily activities and/or work.

121336.explanation: For a large group of people, it is difficult to make ends meet. People on benefits, but also those who are poor despite having a job. Recently, we have already improved provisions for child poverty. Now we continue and want to help everyone up to 140% of the social minimum.

121341.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to encourage electric driving, for both passenger cars and commercial vans and trucks. With this, we want to reduce harmful emissions and improve air quality. We propose establishing an environmental zone for trucks within the inner city ring.

121342.explanation: People should be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them.

121346.explanation: Currently, dog tax is a purpose tax that is poorly enforced. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants to abolish the dog tax.

121347.explanation: The tourist tax in Oosterhout will be raised in 2026 to the regional average, while we have lagged behind for years in the level of our tourist tax. GroenLinks-PvdA supports maintaining an average rate for the tourist tax.

121350.explanation: It is our social duty to shelter refugees and provide them with a home. They only cause a small pressure on the housing market. The pressure on the housing market is mainly relieved by building social rental homes rapidly.

121351.explanation: It is already free to dispose of your waste separately. We want to keep it that way. Disposing of bulky waste costs money and should remain so. This promotes recycling.

121352.explanation: It is important that sufficient sustainable energy is generated in Oosterhout; we must employ all possible methods.

121353.explanation: Currently, there is no school swimming in Oosterhout. GroenLinks-PvdA believes every child should be able to obtain a swimming diploma. Therefore, we want school swimming for all children in group 5 who have not previously earned a swimming certificate.

121354.explanation: In a coffeeshop, you can safely buy preferably legally grown cannabis. Without a coffeeshop, the risk of street dealing is greater.

121355.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA ensured that in Oosterhout at least 80% of the homes to be built must be affordable, of which at least 30% are social housing. The council was overwhelmingly in favor of this. We definitely want to keep this, as the demand for affordable housing is enormous.

121359.explanation: Paid parking in Oosterhout is already much cheaper than in comparable municipalities. We want people to come to the center mainly by bike, public transport, or on foot.

121360.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants fewer parking spaces on the street in the center. This creates more space for greenery, socializing, and outdoor play. Where necessary, we want to invest in a new parking garage, but only if absolutely necessary.

121362.explanation: The Market is a recreational area with many terraces, making cycling there unsafe.

121363.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports increasing access to housing for young people. For many young people, their lives are on hold because they have no home. We build primarily for young people and the elderly. When elderly people find a more suitable home, a house becomes available again for a young family.

121365.explanation: It is our social duty to shelter refugees and comply with the distribution law. It is about contributing our part and housing 250 people. This does not necessarily have to be at one location.

121303.explanation: The Oosterhout VVD supports more women in politics! We focus on the right team capable of addressing Oosterhout's challenges as much as possible. Quality over quantity. Communication skills and collegiality are important. So aiming for 50% is good but not the main thing.

121304.explanation: Thinking along, involving, gathering from outside, listening, connecting with the municipality, creating support, facilitating initiatives, etc., are essential! And can be improved! A referendum is almost always hijacked and polarizes. So consultation, participation, and listening are strongly supported by the VVD.

121307.explanation: Rules must have a purpose. Closing times usually don't help. So let the hospitality sector decide for themselves. The VVD wants that if there is (threatening) nuisance for neighbors, imposing a closing time must be possible. Such as for terraces.

121308.explanation: 'Must' is too strong. There are already many subsidy schemes and often manageable payback periods. Facilitate collaborating entrepreneurs or residents especially with municipal knowledge, skills, and permits. And is there a truly innovative plan? Of course, the VVD does not say no immediately!

121310.explanation: The Oosterhout VVD supports a clear and strict migration policy. The national government is responsible for the measures, not the municipality. We take our responsibility, according to the capacities of our neighborhoods, for those who come to Oosterhout. Housing must always be safe, livable, and focused on participation.

121314.explanation: Organizing a fireworks show is not the municipality's task. The VVD obviously wants that if there is an initiative from entrepreneurs and/or residents, the municipality facilitates and helps. Organize it near the entertainment area so the hospitality sector benefits, and deploy sufficient enforcement officers/police!

121315.explanation: The local liberal VVD remains in favor of cost-covering waste collection. Not free but not more expensive either. Bulky waste can be collected but only at cost price. It would also harm residents who separate waste. We support a circular crafts center and separated collection of sofas.

121322.explanation: A lot of money goes to sports in Oosterhout. That's good! If a sports club is struggling, the municipality should always think along. But... it doesn’t have to immediately give money. Retaining young people is a major challenge. Broad cooperation is needed for a solution. The VVD is willing to invest money in this!

121325.explanation: Definitely! And also flexibly deployable! Dealing, nuisance in parks, shopping centers, etc., are a thorn in the side of the VVD. Camera surveillance can complement the limited capacity of enforcers and police. It works not only repressively but also preventively. Oosterhout must feel and become safer!

121327.explanation: Certainly. The possibility must exist. The VVD believes enforcers themselves should choose whether and when to carry these (batons). Due to the persistent police shortage and the increasing safety role of enforcers, they must have means to stay safe. The baton is part of that.

121332.explanation: This is not the municipality's responsibility. Of course, rules should minimize obstacles, and the municipality must facilitate in the interest of more housing in Oosterhout, but if it is not financially feasible, the municipality should NOT intervene.

121333.explanation: The local VVD wants more suitable homes for young people, including students! Such as above the new public transport hub, the Burcht, etc. We are not in favor of container homes. Just build solid homes! The municipality should not finance this but can, for example, facilitate Thuisvester.

121334.explanation: Work must pay off! Proper guidance to work, study, or (if working is not possible) to meaningful daily activities or volunteer work is a priority! This enhances well-being. And status holders must quickly go to work or study! Social assistance should be a safety net, not a comfortable hammock.

121336.explanation: Working more earns too little. The VVD supports equal opportunities, poverty reduction, and activation toward work/study! The patchwork of regulations helps too little. Ensure that work pays, existing schemes are utilized, and there are fewer rules and forms! So not raising the threshold.

121341.explanation: Even if we were a big city... Oosterhout's air quality is influenced by many factors. Asphalt is the biggest 'polluter.' The highways are also a major factor. It's good that municipal trucks are electric. For the rest, national politics must initiate measures!

121342.explanation: Faith, culture, or belief is a private matter. Care must always be provided with respect for others. This does not mean that people always have rights or can make demands. This does not fit with the liberal values of the VVD.

121346.explanation: Unnecessary taxation and a relatively large portion goes to tax collection. Of course, the VVD supports maintaining the dog parks because they are heavily used.

121347.explanation: The municipal council recently raised this because tourists make extensive use of our facilities, such as the swimming pool, roads, and vibrant city center. The VVD does not want to increase it further now. However, it is worthwhile to see if a larger portion of the revenue can directly benefit local tourism.

121350.explanation: The VVD supports a clear and strict migration policy. The national government is responsible for regulations, not the municipality. If this priority is withdrawn, the municipality must take responsibility. The VVD's position remains: housing must be safe, livable, and aimed at participation.

121351.explanation: The polluter pays the cost price. No more, no less. That is a fair system for passing on costs. It also encourages waste separation and the prevention of residual waste (which is incinerated). The VVD supports a circular craft center to reduce waste costs for residents.

121352.explanation: The local VVD adheres to prior agreements from the RES to install solar panels in the Oranjepolder, even though with current knowledge we might no longer support that. Expanding solar panels on roofs is the best alternative. Let's better use the scarce land in Oosterhout.

121353.explanation: Oosterhout's VVD considers swimming lessons a private responsibility. From the perspective of equal opportunity, we remain in favor of funding swimming lessons via the Leergeld foundation or, if necessary, special social assistance.

121354.explanation: The local VVD was and remains against a coffeeshop. These often cause nuisances, including traffic and loitering. However, we are aware that we must also focus on tackling drug dealers, nitrous oxide use, the sale of strong alcohol to minors, and its abuse. So no coffeeshop, but a firm approach to problems.

121355.explanation: Oosterhout has more than 30% and maintains this rate in new construction. The VVD calls on various neighboring municipalities to expedite reaching a total of 30% social rental housing! It would also help if the national government developed regulations to counteract mismatched living in certain cases.

121359.explanation: Paid parking is a means to better distribute scarce parking spaces, just like unpaid alternatives near the center. It must not become a cash cow! Our rates are not high, so lowering them is unnecessary. Above all, ensure there are enough spots for quick errands.

121360.explanation: So not on the street but under new buildings. Near the center is also helpful, such as under Slotjesveld. Only then can more homes quickly be added downtown, trees can be planted here and there in parking lots, and sufficient disabled parking spaces can be provided.

121362.explanation: Definitely! Due to larger terraces, more events, and changed cyclist behavior, occasional closures are not enforceable. A complete ban from, for example, noon onwards will help with clarity, safety, and ambiance. Also, fatbikes can be immediately addressed!

121363.explanation: Starters and young families have high priority, as do young people! But in certain villages and neighborhoods, it is necessary to enable seniors or single-person households to move on. Or add more expensive homes for a better mix. In short: build more and faster!

121365.explanation: The Oosterhout VVD supports a clear and strict migration policy. The national government is responsible for the measures, not the municipality. We take our responsibility, according to the capacities of our neighborhoods, for those who come to Oosterhout. Housing must always be safe, livable, and focused on participation.

121303.explanation: It is about the right person in the right place in a good mix of men and women.

121304.explanation: Healthy Common Sense places great importance on democratic resilience, where active citizens think along and co-decide. Healthy Common Sense emphasizes that residents should be co-owners of plans. A referendum fits this vision of direct democracy and citizen participation.

121307.explanation: The municipality sets the framework. Within this, cafés and restaurants may determine their own opening hours in consultation with the neighborhood.

121308.explanation: Collective energy generation contributes to economic resilience and makes residents less dependent. The municipality should support this, but indirectly financially. Primarily by facilitating.

121310.explanation: Solidarity cannot be outsourced and people must be there for each other. It must be supported from the grassroots.

121314.explanation: The municipality must facilitate that private initiatives, for example in an association context, can organize a fireworks show.

121322.explanation: Sports clubs have sufficient opportunities themselves to obtain the necessary resources. Moreover, mutual aid should be given more room.

121325.explanation: Structurally increasing camera surveillance is not a solution. The police know exactly where and when extra (temporary) camera surveillance is needed. Give them the space to deploy that then.

121327.explanation: Public Order Officers (BOAs) need better training and better equipment.

121332.explanation: The principle is that developers do not need subsidies for this.

121333.explanation: Our preference is for room rental and other forms of 'infill'.

121334.explanation: We agree that people should voluntarily contribute according to ability. More guidance toward work is a good investment for all parties.

121336.explanation: There are sufficient facilities for people with low incomes to make ends meet.

121341.explanation: We are against introducing an environmental zone in the center of Oosterhout. This is not feasible or affordable for both residents and local SMEs.

121342.explanation: Custom solutions where necessary and possible.

121346.explanation: Dog tax should not be a revenue model for the municipality. Only the actual costs should be charged.

121347.explanation: A good tourism sector is important for Oosterhout's economy.

121351.explanation: We find it reasonable that payment is based on usage.

121352.explanation: Land is scarce and this space must be used better.

121353.explanation: Swimming is important, also for children's safety.

121355.explanation: Preferably more; we need to catch up. Including social owner-occupied homes.

121359.explanation: Plans are to raise rates from 2027. We oppose this. Parking should be cheaper and free in more places.

121360.explanation: There is no place for it.

121362.explanation: We want to encourage people to come by bike.

121365.explanation: We support small-scale childcare, supported by the neighborhood.

121303.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, diversity in governance is important. Quality, expertise, and trust must always come first. A good balance between men and women is desirable, but should not be a strict requirement if it compromises content and administrative strength.

121304.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, it is important that residents have more influence on decisions that directly affect them. A referendum can be a means to this, provided it is used carefully, well explained, and does not replace structural consultation and resident participation.

121307.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we stand for room for entrepreneurship. Entrepreneurs should be able to determine their opening hours within reasonable limits, with attention to livability and consultation with neighbors in case of nuisance.

121308.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we want to actively support residents in sustainability efforts. Collective energy projects contribute to the energy transition, lower costs, and broader support. Municipal support through subsidies or loans fits within this.

121310.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we choose humanity and responsibility. Asylum reception is not desirable but a necessary provision. Closure without an alternative shifts problems elsewhere and does not fit a social municipality.

121314.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, there are benefits to safety and solidarity, but costs, support, and alternatives must be carefully weighed before allocating structural funding.

121315.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, the focus is on waste separation and reuse. Free bulky waste collection lowers barriers, prevents illegal dumping, and contributes to a clean and circular municipality.

121322.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for social cohesion. For Heel Oosterhout, if they face difficulties, the municipality should help preserve facilities and activities for residents.

121325.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety if deployed proportionally. For Heel Oosterhout, it is seen as an additional tool, combined with lighting, prevention, and neighborhood-focused work.

121327.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, de-escalation and proportionality are key. Community enforcement officers (BOA’s) must be visible and approachable; arming them does not fit and may increase tensions.

121332.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, the repurposing of vacant real estate is encouraged. Subsidies can accelerate conversion to housing, which helps against housing shortages and counters vacancy.

121333.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we are open to flexible and modular housing forms in case of housing shortages. Temporary student housing can reduce pressure on the housing market if well integrated.

121334.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, work must be paid, but we are open to voluntary social contributions from welfare recipients where possible. Customization remains fundamental, aimed at a return to regular work and social participation.

121336.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, people just above the current threshold are also struggling. Therefore, we advocate for expansion and more customization in poverty policy.

121341.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, cleaner air and a livable center are priorities. An environmental zone can contribute if introduced carefully with attention to entrepreneurs and residents.

121342.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we stand for inclusive and dignified care. Freedom of choice contributes to appropriate care and trust, as long as quality and accessibility are guaranteed.

121346.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, dog tax is seen as outdated. Abolishing it is fairer and lowers costs for residents without demonstrable disadvantage to the municipality.

121347.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, a healthy balance is desired. Tourism may contribute to amenities, but rates must remain reasonable and not harm the local economy.

121350.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, within the law, priority should be given to people with ties to Oosterhout. However, there must be sufficient housing for everyone.

121351.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, waste disposal must be as accessible as possible. Many types of waste are already free, and residual waste costs should not become too high, so that lower incomes are not burdened and illegal dumping prevented.

121352.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, sun and wind should be balanced with the landscape. Solar panels on agricultural land only with careful consideration and preference for rooftops.

121353.explanation: School swimming is important for safety and equal opportunities. For Heel Oosterhout, every child should learn to swim regardless of income.

121354.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we favor a pragmatic approach. Not opposed in principle, but only allowed if it contributes to safety and limits nuisance.

121355.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, even at least 35% social rental housing is desired. This is necessary for affordability and a balanced housing market.

121359.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we do not advocate lower parking rates but want parking to remain affordable. Cost increases are limited to inflation, so the center remains accessible and attractive for visitors and local small businesses.

121360.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, a balance between accessibility and livability is sought. More parking is not always the solution; smart mobility and traffic flow are just as important.

121362.explanation: For safety and clarity, a bicycle-free Market square makes sense. For Heel Oosterhout, we support measures that improve quality of stay and safety.

121363.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, building for all life stages is important, with extra focus on starters and young families to prevent outmigration. Enough must also be built for seniors to promote housing flow.

121365.explanation: For Heel Oosterhout, we take responsibility regarding asylum seeker reception but carefully consider location and size. Smaller-scale reception in or near residential areas is often better integrated and better for livability.

121303.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that an equal number of men and women in the council ensures fairer, stronger, and future-proof local governance. Naturally, candidates must meet the required qualities.

121304.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that referenda give residents more influence on important choices and thereby strengthen local democracy.

121307.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that hospitality entrepreneurs should have more freedom in their opening hours, within clear frameworks that safeguard livability and safety.

121308.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that residents who want to generate sustainable energy together deserve support because this accelerates the energy transition, strengthens the community, and includes everyone.

121310.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that asylum reception should not be closed but organized well and humanely in cooperation with the national government. The municipality must take responsibility for fulfilling the agreements under the distribution law.

121314.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that a central fireworks show is a safe, sustainable, and unifying alternative to private fireworks use due to the national ban.

121315.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that bulky waste should not be collected for free, because a financial incentive is needed to prevent wastefulness and encourage responsible waste behavior.

121322.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that sports clubs are primarily responsible for their finances but that the municipality should think along and provide targeted support when a club is in genuine financial distress.

121325.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes that camera surveillance should only be expanded where it demonstrably contributes to safety, with respect for privacy and always as part of a broader, neighborhood-focused approach.

121327.explanation: BOAs in Oosterhout should only be equipped with a baton when demonstrably necessary, with proper training and clear conditions, ensuring safety and proportionality remain balanced.

121332.explanation: D66 Oosterhout believes the municipality should deliberately support the transformation of vacant offices into housing, as this contributes quickly, sustainably, and effectively to solving the housing shortage.

121333.explanation: Temporary student housing, such as container homes, helps quickly and flexibly alleviate the housing shortage, and municipal support can make a difference.

121334.explanation: People on social assistance should be encouraged to participate socially, but not through strict obligations: customization, guidance, and perspective work better than coercion.

121336.explanation: Too many local regulations cause inequality and uncertainty; therefore, national standards are needed to keep access to poverty aid fair and stable.

121341.explanation: An environmental zone makes Oosterhout’s city center cleaner, healthier, and more attractive.

121342.explanation: The range of care is limited. People should be able to choose a care provider that aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs wherever possible, as this leads to better, safer, and more dignified care.

121346.explanation: The dog tax in Oosterhout is necessary to be able to create and maintain dog walking areas without other residents having to pay for it.

121347.explanation: The tourist tax should not be reduced because this income is needed to pay for facilities that benefit both tourists and residents.

121350.explanation: D66 Oosterhout provides status holders with the housing they are legally entitled to, but does not want extra priority that increases the waiting time for other housing seekers.

121351.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants to maintain the current system: free drop-off of recyclable waste, and only paying for residual waste at the recycling center.

121352.explanation: D66 Oosterhout prefers solar panels mainly on roofs and paved areas, and only allows solar fields on agricultural land if this can be done carefully, limited, and in a landscape-responsible way.

121353.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants to continue investing in school swimming so that all children can grow up safely, skillfully, and with equal opportunities.

121354.explanation: D66 Oosterhout is open to a coffee shop because regulation is safer, counteracts crime, and causes less nuisance than a ban.

121355.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants at least 32% of all new-build homes to be social rental housing so the city remains affordable and everyone has a fair chance at a home.

121359.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants to make parking in garages cheaper to keep the city center livable, but sees no reason to make street parking cheaper.

121360.explanation: D66 Oosterhout does not want additional street parking spaces but focuses on smart parking in garages and an attractive, low-traffic city center.

121362.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants to make De Markt car-free to make the square safer, quieter, and more attractive as the heart of the city.

121363.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants new construction mainly aimed at first-time buyers and young families so that the housing market moves again and the city remains future-proof, also paying attention to new homes for the elderly to stimulate flow-through.

121365.explanation: D66 Oosterhout wants the municipality to actively seek suitable locations for housing about 250 asylum seekers in a humane, careful, and well-organized manner, as agreed in regional cooperation and the dispersal law.

121303.explanation: The CDA believes that quality should be leading.

121304.explanation: The CDA Oosterhout is in favor of a citizen assembly.

121307.explanation: The CDA does believe the municipality should be able to provide guidelines in this regard, especially concerning closing times.

121308.explanation: The CDA supports citizen initiatives such as energy cooperatives.

121310.explanation: The CDA acknowledges that we have a legal obligation, for which we take our responsibility.

121314.explanation: The CDA believes there may be an alternative for our residents, but that it should not be mandatory.

121315.explanation: The CDA believes that waste disposal should be cost-covering.

121322.explanation: The CDA does believe the municipality has a role in advising and supporting.

121325.explanation: The CDA believes that with regard to camera surveillance, the balance between privacy and safety should be properly maintained.

121327.explanation: The CDA believes that BOAs must be well trained for this and fall under the police’s basic responsibility.

121332.explanation: The CDA considers it important that people can live, but does not want to disrupt the market.

121333.explanation: The CDA believes everyone has the right to decent housing and that the municipality should facilitate this.

121334.explanation: The CDA does not see this as a legal obligation, but does see it as meaningful for participation in society.

121336.explanation: The CDA believes assistance should be offered to residents in poverty regardless of income thresholds.

121341.explanation: The CDA currently sees this as neither feasible nor necessary.

121342.explanation: The CDA supports the possibilities offered by the rules.

121346.explanation: The CDA believes the costs incurred by the municipality in this regard should be covered.

121347.explanation: The CDA believes the tourist tax is at the right level but that the funds should flow back into recreation.

121350.explanation: The CDA believes that everyone in the Netherlands should be treated equally.

121351.explanation: The CDA believes the fees at the recycling center should be cost-covering.

121352.explanation: The CDA believes that the solar ladder should be followed.

121353.explanation: The CDA considers it important that children learn to swim.

121354.explanation: The CDA does not want a coffeeshop in Oosterhout.

121355.explanation: The CDA would like a ratio of 30-40-30.

121359.explanation: The CDA believes that parking should be looked at from the developments of the city and the surrounding cities.

121360.explanation: The CDA believes that the existing parking garages and spaces offer sufficient capacity.

121362.explanation: The CDA believes that the center of Oosterhout must remain accessible and reachable.

121363.explanation: The CDA believes that a good balance in types of housing must be realized, with a focus on starters and young families being important.

121365.explanation: The CDA acknowledges that we have a legal obligation, for which we take our responsibility. It is important that the preconditions are properly defined.

121303.explanation: Unlike this and the previous term, we would like to see women in the executive board. Quality of aldermen always takes precedence, however.

121304.explanation: The instrument of an advisory referendum can be used more often because in this way the voice of Oosterhout residents can be better considered for important themes.

121307.explanation: However, with preservation of minimum quiet times, attention to environmental noise, and safety.

121308.explanation: The choice to generate sustainable energy is an individual choice for which various other forms of subsidy are already available.

121310.explanation: We are against large-scale sheltering of the same groups and in favor of small-scale good shelter for refugees, where integration is well supported and guided.

121314.explanation: From this year on, setting off your own fireworks is prohibited. We understand that for many people fireworks mark the New Year and thus a new beginning. We would therefore like to contribute to this from the municipality, ensuring that the church villages are not forgotten either.

121315.explanation: Provided that you pay the same amount as you would have to pay if you took it yourself to the waste collection point.

121322.explanation: We do not want to differentiate between a sports club or other associations.

121325.explanation: We must ensure that (the footage from) the current camera surveillance can be used more effectively and efficiently. Explicitly explore the limits of privacy legislation. The safety of everyone takes precedence over the privacy of troublemakers.

121327.explanation: The use of striking or bludgeoning weapons should remain reserved for trained people with violence instruction.

121332.explanation: We do believe the municipality should do everything within its possibilities to make it easier to convert offices into housing.

121333.explanation: The municipality does not have (higher) vocational education. Young people from Oosterhout who study elsewhere will mainly look for a room there. Let's ensure it becomes attractive to return after their studies.

121334.explanation: We believe people should be encouraged to become socially active according to their abilities. Research has shown that this leads to more social contacts, which positively affects well-being. The important nuance to this statement is 'according to their abilities'.

121336.explanation: We would also like to see a single line drawn for the various arrangements to make it more transparent when you are entitled to what. It is currently too complicated.

121341.explanation: Oosterhout is located at a motorway junction. This junction provides considerable employment due to its favorable location, also for companies in the center.

121342.explanation: The right to care (or the necessity to receive it) transcends religion, culture, and beliefs. That is why we believe you do not always have a choice.

121346.explanation: No tax is levied on other animals that use public spaces (cats, horses, etc.) either.

121347.explanation: Research has not previously shown that tourists stay away because of the tourist tax alone.

121350.explanation: We are in favor of a better and more transparent housing allocation system so that, among others, young people who are currently applying do not always end up at the back of the queue.

121351.explanation: Regarding current household and kitchen waste. For the rest, we would like to better align the bulky waste tariff with the surrounding municipalities to prevent littering. Construction waste from private individuals should also be deliverable at a reduced rate.

121352.explanation: Let agricultural land remain agricultural land. When it is no longer used for that, consider what the best option is for that specific location: housing, recreation, or returning it to nature.

121353.explanation: There is relatively much water in the Oosterhout area. Not all children automatically receive swimming lessons from home. It is good to ensure that children learn the basic skills to save themselves. In addition, sport is good for body and mind.

121354.explanation: Establishing a coffeeshop negatively affects livability, safety, and social effects. Enforcement incurs high costs.

121355.explanation: Additionally, we believe that housing should be built for people who earn just too much to qualify for social rent.

121359.explanation: Paid parking is a regulatory system, not a revenue model.

121360.explanation: Provided it is smart and targeted. Parking capacity in the center must be increased for residents and short-term parking. It is also good to apply strict parking standards at the new town hall and any additional housing development.

121362.explanation: Especially on weekends, shopping evenings, and at special events. In short, better regulation.

121363.explanation: In recent years, much new construction has been done to promote flow. This is progressing poorly and the demand for housing for starters and young families is high. These young families are also needed in the long term to maintain our level of facilities in the future.

121365.explanation: If this refers to seeking large-scale shelter, LOeS supports small-scale shelter where people are well guided and supported.

statements.title: The Markt in Oosterhout should be made bicycle-free (closed to bicycles).
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Oosterhout.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Paid parking in the centre of Oosterhout should be made cheaper.
The municipality must continue to provide funding for school swimming lessons.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar blijven stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The BOAs who check safety in Oosterhout should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Oosterhout controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: At least 32% of the newly built homes in Oosterhout should be social rental homes. In the Netherlands 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Oosterhout moet minimaal 32% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: New construction should be primarily aimed at first-time buyers and young families.

titleParts.text: Nieuwbouw moet vooral gericht zijn op

titleParts.glossary: People buying a house for the first time who have never had a mortgage.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Movable and temporary homes, usually made from shipping containers.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The residents of Oosterhout should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oosterhout moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
Oosterhout should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the centre.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should look for a location to accommodate approximately 250 asylum seekers.
